controls.showCelestialReferences: Show NCP, SCP, 0h circle, and equator
controls.guideStarPosition: Guide star at right ascension {{ra}} hours, declination {{dec}} degrees
controls.guideStarHelp: Use the arrow keys to move the star, or drag it with the mouse.
controls.showEquatorHorizonAngle: Show angle between celestial equator and horizon

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows the local sky from an observer's horizon, with the cardinal directions and the Sun and stars rising and setting as the sky rotates.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area lets you set the latitude, choose horizon diagram or sky view, face north east south or west, add stars, animate time, show or hide star trails, declination regions, angles, content below the horizon, labels, the meridian, and celestial-pole references, remove all stars, and reset the simulation.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Explore how the sky looks from your location above the horizon.

horizonSystem.currentDetails: The observer is at latitude {{latitude}} degrees. Stars shown: {{starCount}}. The local sidereal time is {{time}} hours.

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows the celestial sphere with the celestial equator, the ecliptic, the celestial poles, and the observer's horizon plane cutting through it.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area lets you set the latitude, switch between the celestial-sphere and horizon views, animate time, show or hide the celestial equator, the 0h circle, the grid, the sphere outline, the horizon plane, the labels, and stars below the horizon, and reset the simulation.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Explore how the celestial sphere is oriented relative to your horizon.

celestialSphere.currentDetails: The observer is at latitude {{latitude}} degrees. The local sidereal time is {{time}} hours.

screenSummary.playArea: The play area combines the horizon diagram and the celestial sphere, letting you change your latitude and advance time to watch the sky rotate.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area lets you add stars, animate time, show or hide star trails, set your location on the map, and reset the simulation.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Adjust the latitude and advance time to explore the rotating sky.

explorer.currentDetails: The observer is at latitude {{latitude}} degrees and longitude {{longitude}} degrees. Stars shown: {{starCount}}. The local sidereal time is {{time}} hours.

keyboardHelp.moveObserverLocationDescription: Arrow keys nudge latitude and longitude on the Earth map.
keyboardHelp.moveGuideStarDescription: Arrow keys nudge right ascension and declination of the guide star.
keyboardHelp.rotateSkyDescription: Arrow keys rotate the camera. Drag with the mouse for the same free rotation.
keyboardHelp.rotateAboutZenithDescription: Alt plus arrow keys spins the view about the vertical axis only. Matches Alt-drag.
keyboardHelp.advanceSiderealTimeDescription: Ctrl plus left or right arrow advances or rewinds sidereal time. Matches Ctrl-drag.
keyboardHelp.moveStarDescription: With a star focused, arrow keys nudge its right ascension and declination.
keyboardHelp.addStarAtCenterDescription: Shift plus Enter adds a star at the center of the focused sky view. Shift-click also adds a star.
keyboardHelp.skyViewHelp: Arrow keys rotate the camera. Alt plus arrows rotate about zenith. Ctrl plus left or right advances sidereal time.
keyboardHelp.starHelp: Arrow keys nudge right ascension and declination. Drag with the mouse to place the star.
